completed_since_last_snapshot: S1.7 Engine loop implemented: src/core/Engine.ts — generic Engine<TWorld> class. tick(realDeltaSeconds) reads current state from StateManager, calls Clock.advance() once, runs the ordered System pipeline once per whole fixed step (tick incremented by exactly 1 per step), commits every resulting snapshot through StateManager, and exposes an interpolationAlpha getter for the renderer. Also commits on a zero-step tick to persist the Clock's banked accumulator (see dm-0007) so leftover real time is never silently lost across calls.

test/unit/Engine.test.ts added: 9 passing tests covering step counting, zero-step accumulator persistence across calls, tick+1-per-step semantics (never skipped/double-counted), system pipeline ordering, StateManager commit continuity (previous/current shift correctly), no in-place mutation of prior snapshots, interpolationAlpha correctness, and the generic/no-gameplay-assumptions contract (works with an empty system list).
Added @types/node as a devDependency (was missing; required for node:test / node:assert/strict typings to compile under strict + noImplicitAny). package.json and package-lock.json updated.

meta/project_knowledge_graph.json updated: new node core/engine (deps: core/clock, core/state, core/state_manager, systems/system; no dependents yet), 4 new DependencyEdges, pending_nodes cleared, Section_16 gdos_anchor extended, pkg_hash bumped s1-c3d4e5 -> s1-e6f7a8.

meta/design_memory_ledger.json: added dm-0007 (why Engine commits even on a zero-step tick — persisting the Clock accumulator through the single-source-of-truth GameState tree rather than a private un-threaded Engine field).

docs/task_slices.md: S1.7 flipped NOT_STARTED -> COMPLETED (not VERIFIED — phase closure is S1.10's job). REQ-121/REQ-160 backlog rows intentionally left as-is: both are multi-phase REQs (REQ-121 spans P1+P2, REQ-160 spans P3+P11) not fully satisfied by this single slice.

meta/active_task.json / meta/runs/: S1.7 tracked through stage manager (reboot->select->plan->anchor->implement->verify->integrate->report all recorded with notes); both acceptance criteria (REQ-121, REQ-160-scoped-to-S1.7) marked met.

exact_execution_focus.pending_logical_transformation: Run S1.8: write test/unit/Vec2.test.ts, Rng.test.ts, Clock.test.ts, State.test.ts, StateManager.test.ts, System.test.ts (Engine.test.ts already exists from S1.7). Each must prove its module's documented contract — Vec2: immutable algebra + no-mutation; Rng: same-seed->same-sequence, no globals; Clock: fixed-step banking correctness + MAX_FRAME_SECONDS spiral-of-death clamp; State: NEUTRAL_INPUT shape + generic-over-world compiles; StateManager: only-mutation-point semantics, prev/current shift, deep-freeze option throws on mutation attempt when enabled, idempotent commit (committing the same object twice is a no-op beyond shifting previous). Then flip S1.1-S1.6 and S1.8 to COMPLETED in docs/task_slices.md once `npm test` is green with full coverage across src/core and src/systems.

blocked_by.reason: No blockers. S1.8 depends only on already-implemented S1.1-S1.7, all of which now typecheck and (for Engine) have passing tests.

critical_warnings_for_next_session: RUN THE REBOOT LIFECYCLE FIRST, as always: read this handoff, confirm pkg_hash s1-e6f7a8 matches meta/project_knowledge_graph.json, then `npm test` (should show 9 passing Engine tests; if red, fix before writing S1.8).

Do NOT change FIXED_STEP_SECONDS in Clock.ts or introduce delta-time scaling — breaks deterministic replay + the future trajectory solver (dm-0003). Engine.ts now depends on this constant being load-bearing; a spiral-of-death/accumulator test in S1.8 must exercise MAX_FRAME_SECONDS clamping without touching this constant.

Do NOT mutate the GameState tree in place. Engine.ts already respects this (every step is a fresh shallow-spread object); S1.8's StateManager tests should include a case with freezeOnCommit:true asserting that attempting in-place mutation of a committed snapshot throws.

Engine commits a snapshot even when Clock.advance() yields 0 steps (dm-0007) — this is intentional and required for accumulator continuity, not a bug. Don't 'simplify' it away in a later refactor without re-reading dm-0007.

REQ-121 and REQ-160 remain NOT fully VERIFIED in docs/requirements_backlog.md — they're multi-phase requirements; only flip them once their full phase scope (REQ-121: P1+P2; REQ-160: P3+P11) is actually done. Don't let S1.7's completion cause a premature backlog flip.
